original_hypothesis.title: TetherToken.deprecate can redirect user transfers/approvals to an arbitrary upgraded contract without interface enforcement, enabling behavioral breaks and potential fund loss
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: USDT-style TetherToken supports an application-level upgrade switch via deprecated/upgradedAddress; when deprecated is true, transfer/transferFrom/approve/balanceOf/allowance/totalSupply calls are forwarded to the upgraded contract. There is no on-chain enforcement that upgradedAddress implements the expected semantics, nor any migration of balances. A malicious owner (or compromised owner key) could set upgradedAddress to a contract that behaves adversarially (e.g., steals balances, lies on balanceOf), breaking user expectations and potentially enabling value extraction in integrated protocols.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While admin abuse is low realism under the threat model (requires owner compromise/collusion), this is a high-impact control point and a common real-world failure mode. Many protocols integrating USDT-like tokens may not anticipate deprecate redirection risks in internal accounting.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function deprecate(address _upgradedAddress) public onlyOwner { deprecated = true; upgradedAddress = _upgradedAddress; }

preconditions: Users/protocols continue interacting with legacy contract after deprecation
Upgraded contract is malicious or incompatible

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: forwarding critical ERC20 methods to arbitrary upgradedAddress
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: no interface/behavior enforcement; legacy storage remains but calls are redirected
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No verification of upgradedAddress codehash/interface; no timelock/cancel mechanism

analysis_questions: Is this USDT contract intended only as a test/mock, or used in production deployments?
Do integrated flows rely on legacy balances post-deprecate?
Are there known constraints on upgradedAddress in deployment governance?
validation_requirements: Assess whether USDT.sol is production-used or test-only in this repository
Model integration impact: protocols holding legacy balances but calling redirected balanceOf/transfer

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If owner key is compromised, attacker sets upgradedAddress to a contract that makes transferByLegacy drain from users or blocks withdrawals, causing widespread fund loss/lock in integrated systems.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potentially catastrophic for systems integrating this token instance
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All holders/integrators of this USDT instance

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests asserting upgradedAddress must conform to expected behavior

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: On-chain monitoring of Deprecate events and upgradedAddress changes

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
